   247→
   248→  fix1 <- fg1[, c("x", "y", "duration")]
   249→  fix2 <- fg2[, c("x", "y", "duration")]
   250→
   251→  colnames(fix1) <- c("start_x", "start_y", "duration")
   252→  colnames(fix2) <- c("start_x", "start_y", "duration")
   253→
   254→  ret <- mmgaze$docomparison(fix1, fix2, as.integer(screensize), grouping=grouping, TDir=tdir,TDur=tdur,TAmp=tamp)
   255→  names(ret) <- c("mm_vector", "mm_direction", "mm_length", "mm_position", "mm_duration")
   256→  ret
   257→}
   258→
   259→#' Compute weighted Earth Mover's Distance (Wasserstein-1) between two 2-D point clouds.
   260→#'
   261→#' This helper tries to use the T4transport package (preferred) and falls back to the
   262→#' transport package if available. Points are supplied as two-column matrices with
   263→#' corresponding non-negative weights that need not sum to one (they will be
   264→#' normalised internally).
   265→#'
   266→#' @param x Matrix of coordinates (n \times 2).
   267→#' @param wx Numeric vector of weights for `x` (length n).
   268→#' @param y Matrix of coordinates (m \times 2).
   269→#' @param wy Numeric vector of weights for `y` (length m).
   270→#' @return A single numeric value – the Earth Mover's Distance.
   271→#' @keywords internal
   272→#' @noRd
   273→emdw <- function(x, wx, y, wy, lambda = 0.01) {
   274→  # Prefer emdist::emdw if available (package already in Imports)
   275→  if (requireNamespace("emdist", quietly = TRUE)) {
   276→    return(emdist::emdw(x, wx, y, wy))
   277→  }
   278→
   279→  # Fall back to T4transport if available
   280→  if (requireNamespace("T4transport", quietly = TRUE)) {
   281→    dmat <- proxy::dist(x, y)
   282→    wx <- wx / sum(wx)
   283→    wy <- wy / sum(wy)
   284→    return(T4transport::sinkhornD(dmat, wx = wx, wy = wy, lambda = lambda)$distance)
   285→  }
   286→
   287→  # Finally, attempt with transport package
   288→  if (requireNamespace("transport", quietly = TRUE)) {
   289→    df1 <- data.frame(x = x[, 1], y = x[, 2], mass = wx / sum(wx))
   290→    df2 <- data.frame(x = y[, 1], y = y[, 2], mass = wy / sum(wy))
   291→    res <- transport::transport(df1, df2, p = 1)
   292→    return(sum(res$dist * res$mass))
   293→  }
   294→
   295→  stop("Could not compute EMD: please install the 'emdist', 'T4transport', or 'transport' package.")
   296→}
   297→
   298→

#' @noRd
emd_position_similarity <- function(fg1, fg2, screensize) {
  # Extract x and y coordinates
  points1 <- as.matrix(fg1[, c("x", "y")])
  points2 <- as.matrix(fg2[, c("x", "y")])

  # Calculate the Earth Mover's Distance
  emd_value <- emdw(points1, fg1$duration, points2, fg2$duration)

  # Normalize EMD to convert it into a similarity metric
  max_dist <- sqrt(screensize[1]^2 + screensize[2]^2)
  normalized_emd <- emd_value / max_dist

  # Convert to similarity
  similarity <- 1 - normalized_emd
  return(similarity)
}


#' @noRd
duration_diff_1d <- function(dur1, dur2, cds) {
  dur1 <- dur1[cds[,1]]
  dur2 <- dur2[cds[,2]]

  adiff <- abs(dur1-dur2)
  denom <- pmax(dur1, dur2)
  adiff/denom
}


#' @noRd
angle_diff_1d <- function(theta1, theta2, cds) {
  theta1 <- theta1[cds[,1]]
  theta2 <- theta2[cds[,2]]

  theta1 <- ifelse(theta1 < 0, pi + (pi + theta1), theta1)
  theta2 <- ifelse(theta2 < 0, pi + (pi + theta2), theta2)

  adiff <- abs(theta1-theta2)
  adiff <- ifelse(adiff > pi, 2 * pi - adiff, adiff)
  adiff
}

#' @noRd
vector_diff_1d <- function(x,y, v1, metric=c("l1", "l2"), cds) {
  metric <- match.arg(metric)
  x1 = x[[v1]]
  x2 = y[[v1]]

  #cds <- arrayInd(as.integer(path), c(nrow(x),nrow(y)))
  #cds <- cbind(cds[,2], cds[,1])

  if (metric == "l2") {
    abs(x1[cds[,1]] - x2[cds[,2]])
  } else {
    x1[cds[,1]] - x2[cds[,2]]
  }

}

#' @noRd
vector_diff_2d <- function(x,y, v1,v2, cds) {
  x1 = x[[v1]]
  x2 = y[[v1]]
  y1 = x[[v2]]
  y2 = y[[v2]]

  #cds <- arrayInd(as.integer(path), c(nrow(x),nrow(y)))
  #cds <- cbind(cds[,2], cds[,1])

  sqrt((x1[cds[,1]] - x2[cds[,2]])^2 + (y1[cds[,1]] - y2[cds[,2]])^2)

}


#' @noRd
create_graph <- function(x, y) {
  M <- proxy::dist(cbind(x$lenx, x$leny), cbind(y$lenx, y$leny))
  M_assignment <- matrix(seq(nrow(M) * ncol(M)), nrow(M), ncol(M), byrow = TRUE)

  nr <- nrow(M)
  nc <- ncol(M)

  edges_right <- if (nc > 1) {
    start <- M_assignment[, -nc, drop = FALSE]
    cbind(as.vector(start), as.vector(start + 1), as.vector(M[, -1, drop = FALSE]))
  } else {
    NULL
  }

  edges_down <- if (nr > 1) {
    start <- M_assignment[-nr, , drop = FALSE]
    cbind(as.vector(start), as.vector(start + nc), as.vector(M[-1, , drop = FALSE]))
  } else {
    NULL
  }

  edges_diag <- if (nr > 1 && nc > 1) {
    start <- M_assignment[-nr, -nc, drop = FALSE]
    cbind(as.vector(start), as.vector(start + nc + 1), as.vector(M[-1, -1, drop = FALSE]))
  } else {
    NULL
  }

  last_node <- M_assignment[nr, nc]
  edges_last <- matrix(c(last_node, last_node, 0), nrow = 1)

  out <- rbind(edges_right, edges_down, edges_diag, edges_last)

  g <- igraph::graph_from_data_frame(out[, 1:2])
  igraph::E(g)$weight <- out[, 3]
  spath <- igraph::shortest_paths(g, 1, max(igraph::V(g)), weights = igraph::E(g)$weight, output = "both", predecessors = TRUE)
  list(g = g, vpath = spath$vpath[[1]], epath = spath$epath[[1]], M = M, M_assignment = M_assignment, pred = spath$predecessors)
}



#' @export
install_multimatch <- function() {
  reticulate::py_install("multimatch_gaze", pip=TRUE)
}


#' Compute MultiMatch Metrics for Scanpath Similarity
#'
#' This function computes multiple similarity metrics between two scanpaths, including vector, direction, length, position, duration, and EMD-based position similarity.
#'
#' @param x A data frame representing the first scanpath. Must contain at least three columns: \code{x}, \code{y}, and \code{onset}, and at least three rows.
#' @param y A data frame representing the second scanpath. Must contain at least three columns: \code{x}, \code{y}, and \code{onset}, and at least three rows.
#' @param screensize A numeric vector of length 2 indicating the width and height of the screen in pixels.
#'
#' @details
#' The function computes six different similarity metrics between the scanpaths \code{x} and \code{y}:
#' \itemize{
#'   \item \code{mm_vector}: Similarity based on the 2D vectors between fixations.
#'   \item \code{mm_direction}: Similarity based on the direction (angle) of saccades between fixations.
#'   \item \code{mm_length}: Similarity based on the length of saccades between fixations.
#'   \item \code{mm_position}: Similarity based on the spatial position of fixations.
#'   \item \code{mm_duration}: Similarity based on the duration of fixations.
#'   \item \code{mm_position_emd}: Order-insensitive similarity based on the Earth Mover's Distance (EMD) between the spatial positions of fixations.
#' }
#'
#' The function ensures that both scanpaths have strictly increasing onset times and contain at least three fixations. It also normalizes the similarity scores to lie between 0 and 1, with higher values indicating greater similarity.
#'
#' @return A named numeric vector with the following elements:
#' \describe{
#'   \item{mm_vector}{Similarity based on the 2D vectors between fixations.}
#'   \item{mm_direction}{Similarity based on the direction (angle) of saccades between fixations.}
#'   \item{mm_length}{Similarity based on the length of saccades between fixations.}
#'   \item{mm_position}{Similarity based on the spatial position of fixations.}
#'   \item{mm_duration}{Similarity based on the duration of fixations.}
#'   \item{mm_position_emd}{Order-insensitive similarity based on the Earth Mover's Distance (EMD) between the spatial positions of fixations.}
#' }
#'
#' @references
#' Dewhurst, R., Nyström, M., Jarodzka, H., Foulsham, T., Johansson, R., & Holmqvist, K. (2012).
#' It depends on how you look at it: Scanpath comparison in multiple dimensions with MultiMatch, a vector-based approach.
#' Behavior research methods, 44, 1079-1100.
#'
#' @examples
#' \dontrun{
#' # Example usage:
#' scanpath1 <- data.frame(x = runif(10, 0, 500), y = runif(10, 0, 500), onset = cumsum(runif(10, 1, 5)))
#' scanpath2 <- data.frame(x = runif(10, 0, 500), y = runif(10, 0, 500), onset = cumsum(runif(10, 1, 5)))
#' screensize <- c(500, 500)
#' similarity_scores <- multi_match(scanpath1, scanpath2, screensize)
#' print(similarity_scores)
#' }
#'
#' @importFrom dplyr arrange
#' @importFrom stats median
#' @export
multi_match <- function(x,y, screensize) {

  if (any(diff(x$onset) <= 0)) {
    stop("multi_match: x `onset` vector must be strictly increasing")
  }

  if (any(diff(y$onset) <= 0)) {
    stop("multi_match: y `onset` vector must be strictly increasing")
  }

  if (nrow(x) < 3 || nrow(y) < 3) {
    warning("multi_match requires 3 or more coordinates in each scanpath, returning NAs")
    return(c(mm_vector=NA, mm_direction=NA,
             mm_length=NA, mm_position=NA,
             mm_duration=NA))

  }

  sacx <- x[1:(nrow(x)-1),]
  sacy <- y[1:(nrow(y)-1),]

  gout <- create_graph(sacx,sacy)
  p <- as.integer(gout$vpath)
  rnum <- ceiling(p / ncol(gout$M))
  cnum <-  p %% ncol(gout$M)
  cnum[cnum==0] <- ncol(gout$M)

  cds <- cbind(rnum, cnum)

  vector_d <- vector_diff_2d(sacx, sacy, "lenx", "leny", cds)
  vector_sim <-  1- median(vector_d) / (2 * sqrt(screensize[1]^2 + (screensize[2]^2)))

  direction_d <- angle_diff_1d(sacx$theta, sacy$theta, cds)
  direction_sim <- 1 - median(direction_d) / pi

  duration_d <- duration_diff_1d(sacx$duration, sacy$duration, cds)
  duration_sim <- 1 - median(duration_d)

  length_d <- abs(vector_diff_1d(sacx, sacy, "rho", metric="l1", cds))
  length_sim <- 1 - (median(length_d)) / (sqrt(screensize[1]^2 + (screensize[2]^2)))

  position_d <- vector_diff_2d(sacx, sacy, "x", "y", cds)
  position_sim <- 1 - (median(position_d)) / (sqrt(screensize[1]^2 + (screensize[2]^2)))

  emd_position_sim <- emd_position_similarity(sacx, sacy, screensize)

  c(mm_vector=vector_sim, mm_direction=direction_sim,
    mm_length=length_sim, mm_position=position_sim,
    mm_duration=duration_sim,
    mm_position_emd=emd_position_sim)
}


#' @noRd
py_multi_match <- function(fg1, fg2,
                        screensize,
                        grouping=FALSE,
                        tdir=25,
                        tdur=.05,
                        tamp=100) {

  if (!requireNamespace("reticulate")) {
    stop("multi_match requires access to python library `multimatch_gaze` via `reticulate`, please install")
  }

  if (!exists("mmgaze")) {
    mmgaze <<- try(reticulate::import("multimatch_gaze"))
    if (inherits(mmgaze, "try-error")) {
      stop("cannot load python module `multimatch_gaze`")
    }
  }

  fg1 <- fg1 %>% arrange(onset)
  fg2 <- fg2 %>% arrange(onset)

  fix1 <- fg1[, c("x", "y", "duration")]
  fix2 <- fg2[, c("x", "y", "duration")]

  colnames(fix1) <- c("start_x", "start_y", "duration")
  colnames(fix2) <- c("start_x", "start_y", "duration")

  ret <- mmgaze$docomparison(fix1, fix2, as.integer(screensize), grouping=grouping, TDir=tdir,TDur=tdur,TAmp=tamp)
  names(ret) <- c("mm_vector", "mm_direction", "mm_length", "mm_position", "mm_duration")
  ret
}

#' Compute weighted Earth Mover's Distance (Wasserstein-1) between two 2-D point clouds.
#'
#' This helper tries to use the T4transport package (preferred) and falls back to the
#' transport package if available. Points are supplied as two-column matrices with
#' corresponding non-negative weights that need not sum to one (they will be
#' normalised internally).
#'
#' @param x Matrix of coordinates (n \times 2).
#' @param wx Numeric vector of weights for `x` (length n).
#' @param y Matrix of coordinates (m \times 2).
#' @param wy Numeric vector of weights for `y` (length m).
#' @return A single numeric value – the Earth Mover's Distance.
#' @keywords internal
#' @noRd
emdw <- function(x, wx, y, wy, lambda = 0.01) {
  # Prefer emdist::emdw if available (package already in Imports)
  if (requireNamespace("emdist", quietly = TRUE)) {
    return(emdist::emdw(x, wx, y, wy))
  }

  # Fall back to T4transport if available
  if (requireNamespace("T4transport", quietly = TRUE)) {
    dmat <- proxy::dist(x, y)
    wx <- wx / sum(wx)
    wy <- wy / sum(wy)
    return(T4transport::sinkhornD(dmat, wx = wx, wy = wy, lambda = lambda)$distance)
  }

  # Finally, attempt with transport package
  if (requireNamespace("transport", quietly = TRUE)) {
    df1 <- data.frame(x = x[, 1], y = x[, 2], mass = wx / sum(wx))
    df2 <- data.frame(x = y[, 1], y = y[, 2], mass = wy / sum(wy))
    res <- transport::transport(df1, df2, p = 1)
    return(sum(res$dist * res$mass))
  }

  stop("Could not compute EMD: please install the 'emdist', 'T4transport', or 'transport' package.")
}

input.content:
"""
Core similarity metrics for eye tracking data.

This module provides various similarity metrics for comparing eye tracking data
including fixation groups, density maps, and scanpaths.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Union, Optional, List, Dict, Any
import numpy as np
from numpy.typing import NDArray
import warnings
from functools import singledispatch

from peyesim.core.fixation_group import FixationGroup
from peyesim.core.eye_density import EyeDensity


@singledispatch
def similarity(x: Any, y: Any, method: str = "pearson", **kwargs) -> Union[float, NDArray]:
    """
    Compute similarity between two eye tracking data objects.
    
    This is a generic function that dispatches to specific implementations
    based on the type of the first argument.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    x : object
        First eye tracking data object
    y : object
        Second eye tracking data object
    method : str, default="pearson"
        Similarity metric to use
    **kwargs
        Additional arguments passed to the specific similarity function
        
    Returns
    -------
    float or ndarray
        Similarity score(s)
    """
    raise NotImplementedError(f"No similarity method for type {type(x)}")


@similarity.register(EyeDensity)
def similarity_density(
    x: EyeDensity,
    y: EyeDensity,
    method: str = "pearson",
    saliency_map: Optional[Union[EyeDensity, NDArray]] = None,
    **kwargs
) -> float:
    """
    Compute similarity between two density maps.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    x : EyeDensity
        First density map
    y : EyeDensity
        Second density map
    method : str, default="pearson"
        Similarity metric. Options:
        - "pearson": Pearson correlation
        - "spearman": Spearman correlation
        - "fisherz": Fisher z-transformed Pearson correlation
        - "cosine": Cosine similarity
        - "l1": L1 similarity (1 - 0.5 * L1_distance)
        - "jaccard": Jaccard similarity
        - "dcov": Distance correlation
        - "emd": Earth Mover's Distance based similarity
    saliency_map : EyeDensity or ndarray, optional
        Saliency map for EMD computation with saliency weighting
        
    Returns
    -------
    float
        Similarity score
    """
    if method == "emd":
        return compute_similarity(x, y, method=method, saliency_map=saliency_map)
    else:
        if isinstance(y, EyeDensity):
            y_data = y.z
        else:
            y_data = y
        return compute_similarity(x.z, y_data, method=method)


def compute_similarity(
    x: Union[NDArray, EyeDensity],
    y: Union[NDArray, EyeDensity],
    method: str = "pearson",
    saliency_map: Optional[Union[EyeDensity, NDArray]] = None
) -> float:
    """
    Compute similarity between two arrays or density maps.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    x : ndarray or EyeDensity
        First data array or density map
    y : ndarray or EyeDensity
        Second data array or density map
    method : str, default="pearson"
        Similarity metric to use
    saliency_map : EyeDensity or ndarray, optional
        Saliency map for EMD computation
        
    Returns
    -------
    float
        Similarity score
    """
    if method == "emd":
        if not (hasattr(x, 'x') and hasattr(x, 'y') and hasattr(x, 'z')):
            raise ValueError("method 'emd' requires eye_density objects with x, y, z components")
        if not (hasattr(y, 'x') and hasattr(y, 'y') and hasattr(y, 'z')):
            raise ValueError("method 'emd' requires eye_density objects with x, y, z components")
            
        # Create coordinate grid
        coords = np.column_stack([
            np.repeat(x.x, len(x.y)),
            np.tile(x.y, len(x.x))
        ])
        
        wx = x.z.ravel()
        wy = y.z.ravel()
        
        if saliency_map is not None:
            # Extract saliency matrix
            s_mat = saliency_map.z if hasattr(saliency_map, 'z') else saliency_map
            if s_mat.shape != x.z.shape:
                raise ValueError("saliency_map dimensions must match density maps")
                
            # Compute residuals
            r1 = x.z - s_mat
            r2 = y.z - s_mat
            
            # Split into positive and negative parts
            pos1 = np.maximum(r1, 0)
            neg1 = np.maximum(-r1, 0)
            pos2 = np.maximum(r2, 0)
            neg2 = np.maximum(-r2, 0)
            
            # Compute EMD for positive and negative parts
            try:
                from peyesim.utils.emd import emdw
                emd_pos = emdw(coords, pos1.ravel(), coords, pos2.ravel())
                emd_neg = emdw(coords, neg1.ravel(), coords, neg2.ravel())
                return -(emd_pos + emd_neg)
            except ImportError:
                raise ImportError("EMD computation requires 'emdist' package")
        else:
            try:
                from peyesim.utils.emd import emdw
                emd_dist = emdw(coords, wx, coords, wy)
                return 1 / (1 + emd_dist)
            except ImportError:
                raise ImportError("EMD computation requires 'emdist' package")
    
    # Convert to vectors
    vx = np.asarray(x).ravel()
    vy = np.asarray(y).ravel()
    
    # Check for sufficient non-NA data
    valid_x = ~np.isnan(vx)
    valid_y = ~np.isnan(vy)
    valid_common = valid_x & valid_y
    
    if np.sum(valid_common) < 2:
        warnings.warn("Less than 2 common valid data points for similarity calculation.")
        return np.nan
        
    vx_common = vx[valid_common]
    vy_common = vy[valid_common]
    
    # Check variance for correlation methods
    var_x = np.var(vx_common)
    var_y = np.var(vy_common)
    
    if method in ["pearson", "spearman", "fisherz", "dcov"]:
        is_zero_var_x = np.isnan(var_x) or var_x < np.finfo(float).eps
        is_zero_var_y = np.isnan(var_y) or var_y < np.finfo(float).eps
        
        if is_zero_var_x or is_zero_var_y:
            # Check if vectors are identical
            if (is_zero_var_x and is_zero_var_y and 
                np.allclose(vx_common, vy_common, atol=np.sqrt(np.finfo(float).eps))):
                return 1.0  # Perfect correlation for identical vectors
            else:
                warnings.warn(f"Method {method} requires variance in both inputs, "
                            "or identical inputs if variance is zero.")
                return np.nan
    
    if method in ["pearson", "spearman"]:
        return np.corrcoef(vx_common, vy_common)[0, 1] if method == "pearson" else _spearman_corr(vx_common, vy_common)
    
    elif method == "fisherz":
        cor_val = np.corrcoef(vx_common, vy_common)[0, 1]
        # Ensure cor_val is within (-1, 1) for arctanh
        cor_val = np.clip(cor_val, -1 + np.finfo(float).eps, 1 - np.finfo(float).eps)
        return np.arctanh(cor_val)
    
    elif method == "cosine":
        # Cosine similarity
        norm_x = np.linalg.norm(vx_common)
        norm_y = np.linalg.norm(vy_common)
        if norm_x == 0 or norm_y == 0:
            warnings.warn("Cannot compute cosine similarity with zero vector")
            return np.nan
        return np.dot(vx_common, vy_common) / (norm_x * norm_y)
    
    elif method == "l1":
        # L1 similarity
        if np.any(vx_common < 0) or np.any(vy_common < 0):
            warnings.warn("L1 distance interpretation assumes non-negative densities.")
            
        sum_x = np.sum(vx_common)
        sum_y = np.sum(vy_common)
        
        if sum_x <= np.finfo(float).eps or sum_y <= np.finfo(float).eps:
            warnings.warn("Cannot normalize for L1 distance; sum is too small.")
            return np.nan
            
        x1 = vx_common / sum_x
        x2 = vy_common / sum_y
        
        # L1 distance is sum(|x1_i - x2_i|). Similarity = 1 - 0.5 * L1_distance
        l1_dist = np.sum(np.abs(x1 - x2))
        return 1 - 0.5 * l1_dist
    
    elif method == "jaccard":
        # Extended Jaccard similarity
        min_sum = np.sum(np.minimum(vx_common, vy_common))
        max_sum = np.sum(np.maximum(vx_common, vy_common))
        
        if max_sum <= np.finfo(float).eps:
            warnings.warn("Cannot compute Jaccard similarity; max sum is too small.")
            return np.nan
            
        return min_sum / max_sum
    
    elif method == "dcov":
        # Distance correlation
        sum_x = np.sum(vx_common)
        sum_y = np.sum(vy_common)
        
        if sum_x <= np.finfo(float).eps or sum_y <= np.finfo(float).eps:
            warnings.warn("Cannot normalize for dcov; sum is too small.")
            return np.nan
            
        x1 = vx_common / sum_x
        x2 = vy_common / sum_y
        
        try:
            return _distance_correlation(x1, x2)
        except Exception as e:
            warnings.warn(f"Error computing distance correlation: {e}")
            return np.nan
    
    else:
        raise ValueError(f"Unknown similarity method: {method}")


def _spearman_corr(x: NDArray, y: NDArray) -> float:
    """Compute Spearman correlation coefficient."""
    from scipy import stats
    return stats.spearmanr(x, y)[0]


def _distance_correlation(x: NDArray, y: NDArray) -> float:
    """
    Compute distance correlation between two arrays.
    
    Simple implementation of distance correlation.
    For better performance, consider using the 'dcor' package.
    """
    n = len(x)
    if n < 2:
        return np.nan
        
    # Compute distance matrices
    x_dist = np.abs(x[:, np.newaxis] - x[np.newaxis, :])
    y_dist = np.abs(y[:, np.newaxis] - y[np.newaxis, :])
    
    # Double center the distance matrices
    x_dist_centered = x_dist - x_dist.mean(axis=0) - x_dist.mean(axis=1)[:, np.newaxis] + x_dist.mean()
    y_dist_centered = y_dist - y_dist.mean(axis=0) - y_dist.mean(axis=1)[:, np.newaxis] + y_dist.mean()
    
    # Compute distance covariance and variances
    dcov_xy = np.sqrt(np.mean(x_dist_centered * y_dist_centered))
    dvar_x = np.sqrt(np.mean(x_dist_centered * x_dist_centered))
    dvar_y = np.sqrt(np.mean(y_dist_centered * y_dist_centered))
    
    if dvar_x == 0 or dvar_y == 0:
        return 0.0
        
    return dcov_xy / np.sqrt(dvar_x * dvar_y)


# Additional similarity functions for fixation groups will be added here

"""
Core similarity metrics for eye tracking data.

This module provides various similarity metrics for comparing eye tracking data
including fixation groups, density maps, and scanpaths.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Union, Optional, List, Dict, Any
import numpy as np
from numpy.typing import NDArray
import warnings
from functools import singledispatch

from peyesim.core.fixation_group import FixationGroup
from peyesim.core.eye_density import EyeDensity


@singledispatch
def similarity(x: Any, y: Any, method: str = "pearson", **kwargs) -> Union[float, NDArray]:
    """
    Compute similarity between two eye tracking data objects.
    
    This is a generic function that dispatches to specific implementations
    based on the type of the first argument.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    x : object
        First eye tracking data object
    y : object
        Second eye tracking data object
    method : str, default="pearson"
        Similarity metric to use
    **kwargs
        Additional arguments passed to the specific similarity function
        
    Returns
    -------
    float or ndarray
        Similarity score(s)
    """
    raise NotImplementedError(f"No similarity method for type {type(x)}")


@similarity.register(EyeDensity)
def similarity_density(
    x: EyeDensity,
    y: EyeDensity,
    method: str = "pearson",
    saliency_map: Optional[Union[EyeDensity, NDArray]] = None,
    **kwargs
) -> float:
    """
    Compute similarity between two density maps.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    x : EyeDensity
        First density map
    y : EyeDensity
        Second density map
    method : str, default="pearson"
        Similarity metric. Options:
        - "pearson": Pearson correlation
        - "spearman": Spearman correlation
        - "fisherz": Fisher z-transformed Pearson correlation
        - "cosine": Cosine similarity
        - "l1": L1 similarity (1 - 0.5 * L1_distance)
        - "jaccard": Jaccard similarity
        - "dcov": Distance correlation
        - "emd": Earth Mover's Distance based similarity
    saliency_map : EyeDensity or ndarray, optional
        Saliency map for EMD computation with saliency weighting
        
    Returns
    -------
    float
        Similarity score
    """
    if method == "emd":
        return compute_similarity(x, y, method=method, saliency_map=saliency_map)
    else:
        if isinstance(y, EyeDensity):
            y_data = y.z
        else:
            y_data = y
        return compute_similarity(x.z, y_data, method=method)


def compute_similarity(
    x: Union[NDArray, EyeDensity],
    y: Union[NDArray, EyeDensity],
    method: str = "pearson",
    saliency_map: Optional[Union[EyeDensity, NDArray]] = None
) -> float:
    """
    Compute similarity between two arrays or density maps.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    x : ndarray or EyeDensity
        First data array or density map
    y : ndarray or EyeDensity
        Second data array or density map
    method : str, default="pearson"
        Similarity metric to use
    saliency_map : EyeDensity or ndarray, optional
        Saliency map for EMD computation
        
    Returns
    -------
    float
        Similarity score
    """
    if method == "emd":
        if not (hasattr(x, 'x') and hasattr(x, 'y') and hasattr(x, 'z')):
            raise ValueError("method 'emd' requires eye_density objects with x, y, z components")
        if not (hasattr(y, 'x') and hasattr(y, 'y') and hasattr(y, 'z')):
            raise ValueError("method 'emd' requires eye_density objects with x, y, z components")
            
        # Create coordinate grid
        coords = np.column_stack([
            np.repeat(x.x, len(x.y)),
            np.tile(x.y, len(x.x))
        ])
        
        wx = x.z.ravel()
        wy = y.z.ravel()
        
        if saliency_map is not None:
            # Extract saliency matrix
            s_mat = saliency_map.z if hasattr(saliency_map, 'z') else saliency_map
            if s_mat.shape != x.z.shape:
                raise ValueError("saliency_map dimensions must match density maps")
                
            # Compute residuals
            r1 = x.z - s_mat
            r2 = y.z - s_mat
            
            # Split into positive and negative parts
            pos1 = np.maximum(r1, 0)
            neg1 = np.maximum(-r1, 0)
            pos2 = np.maximum(r2, 0)
            neg2 = np.maximum(-r2, 0)
            
            # Compute EMD for positive and negative parts
            try:
                from peyesim.utils.emd import emdw
                emd_pos = emdw(coords, pos1.ravel(), coords, pos2.ravel())
                emd_neg = emdw(coords, neg1.ravel(), coords, neg2.ravel())
                return -(emd_pos + emd_neg)
            except ImportError:
                raise ImportError("EMD computation requires 'emdist' package")
        else:
            try:
                from peyesim.utils.emd import emdw
                emd_dist = emdw(coords, wx, coords, wy)
                return 1 / (1 + emd_dist)
            except ImportError:
                raise ImportError("EMD computation requires 'emdist' package")
    
    # Convert to vectors
    vx = np.asarray(x).ravel()
    vy = np.asarray(y).ravel()
    
    # Check for sufficient non-NA data
    valid_x = ~np.isnan(vx)
    valid_y = ~np.isnan(vy)
    valid_common = valid_x & valid_y
    
    if np.sum(valid_common) < 2:
        warnings.warn("Less than 2 common valid data points for similarity calculation.")
        return np.nan
        
    vx_common = vx[valid_common]
    vy_common = vy[valid_common]
    
    # Check variance for correlation methods
    var_x = np.var(vx_common)
    var_y = np.var(vy_common)
    
    if method in ["pearson", "spearman", "fisherz", "dcov"]:
        is_zero_var_x = np.isnan(var_x) or var_x < np.finfo(float).eps
        is_zero_var_y = np.isnan(var_y) or var_y < np.finfo(float).eps
        
        if is_zero_var_x or is_zero_var_y:
            # Check if vectors are identical
            if (is_zero_var_x and is_zero_var_y and 
                np.allclose(vx_common, vy_common, atol=np.sqrt(np.finfo(float).eps))):
                return 1.0  # Perfect correlation for identical vectors
            else:
                warnings.warn(f"Method {method} requires variance in both inputs, "
                            "or identical inputs if variance is zero.")
                return np.nan
    
    if method in ["pearson", "spearman"]:
        return np.corrcoef(vx_common, vy_common)[0, 1] if method == "pearson" else _spearman_corr(vx_common, vy_common)
    
    elif method == "fisherz":
        cor_val = np.corrcoef(vx_common, vy_common)[0, 1]
        # Ensure cor_val is within (-1, 1) for arctanh
        cor_val = np.clip(cor_val, -1 + np.finfo(float).eps, 1 - np.finfo(float).eps)
        return np.arctanh(cor_val)
    
    elif method == "cosine":
        # Cosine similarity
        norm_x = np.linalg.norm(vx_common)
        norm_y = np.linalg.norm(vy_common)
        if norm_x == 0 or norm_y == 0:
            warnings.warn("Cannot compute cosine similarity with zero vector")
            return np.nan
        return np.dot(vx_common, vy_common) / (norm_x * norm_y)
    
    elif method == "l1":
        # L1 similarity
        if np.any(vx_common < 0) or np.any(vy_common < 0):
            warnings.warn("L1 distance interpretation assumes non-negative densities.")
            
        sum_x = np.sum(vx_common)
        sum_y = np.sum(vy_common)
        
        if sum_x <= np.finfo(float).eps or sum_y <= np.finfo(float).eps:
            warnings.warn("Cannot normalize for L1 distance; sum is too small.")
            return np.nan
            
        x1 = vx_common / sum_x
        x2 = vy_common / sum_y
        
        # L1 distance is sum(|x1_i - x2_i|). Similarity = 1 - 0.5 * L1_distance
        l1_dist = np.sum(np.abs(x1 - x2))
        return 1 - 0.5 * l1_dist
    
    elif method == "jaccard":
        # Extended Jaccard similarity
        min_sum = np.sum(np.minimum(vx_common, vy_common))
        max_sum = np.sum(np.maximum(vx_common, vy_common))
        
        if max_sum <= np.finfo(float).eps:
            warnings.warn("Cannot compute Jaccard similarity; max sum is too small.")
            return np.nan
            
        return min_sum / max_sum
    
    elif method == "dcov":
        # Distance correlation
        sum_x = np.sum(vx_common)
        sum_y = np.sum(vy_common)
        
        if sum_x <= np.finfo(float).eps or sum_y <= np.finfo(float).eps:
            warnings.warn("Cannot normalize for dcov; sum is too small.")
            return np.nan
            
        x1 = vx_common / sum_x
        x2 = vy_common / sum_y
        
        try:
            return _distance_correlation(x1, x2)
        except Exception as e:
            warnings.warn(f"Error computing distance correlation: {e}")
            return np.nan
    
    else:
        raise ValueError(f"Unknown similarity method: {method}")


def _spearman_corr(x: NDArray, y: NDArray) -> float:
    """Compute Spearman correlation coefficient."""
    from scipy import stats
    return stats.spearmanr(x, y)[0]


def _distance_correlation(x: NDArray, y: NDArray) -> float:
    """
    Compute distance correlation between two arrays.
    
    Simple implementation of distance correlation.
    For better performance, consider using the 'dcor' package.
    """
    n = len(x)
    if n < 2:
        return np.nan
        
    # Compute distance matrices
    x_dist = np.abs(x[:, np.newaxis] - x[np.newaxis, :])
    y_dist = np.abs(y[:, np.newaxis] - y[np.newaxis, :])
    
    # Double center the distance matrices
    x_dist_centered = x_dist - x_dist.mean(axis=0) - x_dist.mean(axis=1)[:, np.newaxis] + x_dist.mean()
    y_dist_centered = y_dist - y_dist.mean(axis=0) - y_dist.mean(axis=1)[:, np.newaxis] + y_dist.mean()
    
    # Compute distance covariance and variances
    dcov_xy = np.sqrt(np.mean(x_dist_centered * y_dist_centered))
    dvar_x = np.sqrt(np.mean(x_dist_centered * x_dist_centered))
    dvar_y = np.sqrt(np.mean(y_dist_centered * y_dist_centered))
    
    if dvar_x == 0 or dvar_y == 0:
        return 0.0
        
    return dcov_xy / np.sqrt(dvar_x * dvar_y)


# Additional similarity functions for fixation groups will be added here

"""
Core similarity metrics for eye tracking data.

This module provides various similarity metrics for comparing eye tracking data
including fixation groups, density maps, and scanpaths.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Union, Optional, List, Dict, Any
import numpy as np
from numpy.typing import NDArray
import warnings
from functools import singledispatch

from peyesim.core.fixation_group import FixationGroup
from peyesim.core.eye_density import EyeDensity


@singledispatch
def similarity(x: Any, y: Any, method: str = "pearson", **kwargs) -> Union[float, NDArray]:
    """
    Compute similarity between two eye tracking data objects.
    
    This is a generic function that dispatches to specific implementations
    based on the type of the first argument.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    x : object
        First eye tracking data object
    y : object
        Second eye tracking data object
    method : str, default="pearson"
        Similarity metric to use
    **kwargs
        Additional arguments passed to the specific similarity function
        
    Returns
    -------
    float or ndarray
        Similarity score(s)
    """
    raise NotImplementedError(f"No similarity method for type {type(x)}")


@similarity.register(EyeDensity)
def similarity_density(
    x: EyeDensity,
    y: EyeDensity,
    method: str = "pearson",
    saliency_map: Optional[Union[EyeDensity, NDArray]] = None,
    **kwargs
) -> float:
    """
    Compute similarity between two density maps.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    x : EyeDensity
        First density map
    y : EyeDensity
        Second density map
    method : str, default="pearson"
        Similarity metric. Options:
        - "pearson": Pearson correlation
        - "spearman": Spearman correlation
        - "fisherz": Fisher z-transformed Pearson correlation
        - "cosine": Cosine similarity
        - "l1": L1 similarity (1 - 0.5 * L1_distance)
        - "jaccard": Jaccard similarity
        - "dcov": Distance correlation
        - "emd": Earth Mover's Distance based similarity
    saliency_map : EyeDensity or ndarray, optional
        Saliency map for EMD computation with saliency weighting
        
    Returns
    -------
    float
        Similarity score
    """
    if method == "emd":
        return compute_similarity(x, y, method=method, saliency_map=saliency_map)
    else:
        if isinstance(y, EyeDensity):
            y_data = y.z
        else:
            y_data = y
        return compute_similarity(x.z, y_data, method=method)


def compute_similarity(
    x: Union[NDArray, EyeDensity],
    y: Union[NDArray, EyeDensity],
    method: str = "pearson",
    saliency_map: Optional[Union[EyeDensity, NDArray]] = None
) -> float:
    """
    Compute similarity between two arrays or density maps.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    x : ndarray or EyeDensity
        First data array or density map
    y : ndarray or EyeDensity
        Second data array or density map
    method : str, default="pearson"
        Similarity metric to use
    saliency_map : EyeDensity or ndarray, optional
        Saliency map for EMD computation
        
    Returns
    -------
    float
        Similarity score
    """
    if method == "emd":
        if not (hasattr(x, 'x') and hasattr(x, 'y') and hasattr(x, 'z')):
            raise ValueError("method 'emd' requires eye_density objects with x, y, z components")
        if not (hasattr(y, 'x') and hasattr(y, 'y') and hasattr(y, 'z')):
            raise ValueError("method 'emd' requires eye_density objects with x, y, z components")
            
        # Create coordinate grid
        coords = np.column_stack([
            np.repeat(x.x, len(x.y)),
            np.tile(x.y, len(x.x))
        ])
        
        wx = x.z.ravel()
        wy = y.z.ravel()
        
        if saliency_map is not None:
            # Extract saliency matrix
            s_mat = saliency_map.z if hasattr(saliency_map, 'z') else saliency_map
            if s_mat.shape != x.z.shape:
                raise ValueError("saliency_map dimensions must match density maps")
                
            # Compute residuals
            r1 = x.z - s_mat
            r2 = y.z - s_mat
            
            # Split into positive and negative parts
            pos1 = np.maximum(r1, 0)
            neg1 = np.maximum(-r1, 0)
            pos2 = np.maximum(r2, 0)
            neg2 = np.maximum(-r2, 0)
            
            # Compute EMD for positive and negative parts
            try:
                from peyesim.utils.emd import emdw
                emd_pos = emdw(coords, pos1.ravel(), coords, pos2.ravel())
                emd_neg = emdw(coords, neg1.ravel(), coords, neg2.ravel())
                return -(emd_pos + emd_neg)
            except ImportError:
                raise ImportError("EMD computation requires 'emdist' package")
        else:
            try:
                from peyesim.utils.emd import emdw
                emd_dist = emdw(coords, wx, coords, wy)
                return 1 / (1 + emd_dist)
            except ImportError:
                raise ImportError("EMD computation requires 'emdist' package")
    
    # Convert to vectors
    vx = np.asarray(x).ravel()
    vy = np.asarray(y).ravel()
    
    # Check for sufficient non-NA data
    valid_x = ~np.isnan(vx)
    valid_y = ~np.isnan(vy)
    valid_common = valid_x & valid_y
    
    if np.sum(valid_common) < 2:
        warnings.warn("Less than 2 common valid data points for similarity calculation.")
        return np.nan
        
    vx_common = vx[valid_common]
    vy_common = vy[valid_common]
    
    # Check variance for correlation methods
    var_x = np.var(vx_common)
    var_y = np.var(vy_common)
    
    if method in ["pearson", "spearman", "fisherz", "dcov"]:
        is_zero_var_x = np.isnan(var_x) or var_x < np.finfo(float).eps
        is_zero_var_y = np.isnan(var_y) or var_y < np.finfo(float).eps
        
        if is_zero_var_x or is_zero_var_y:
            # Check if vectors are identical
            if (is_zero_var_x and is_zero_var_y and 
                np.allclose(vx_common, vy_common, atol=np.sqrt(np.finfo(float).eps))):
                return 1.0  # Perfect correlation for identical vectors
            else:
                warnings.warn(f"Method {method} requires variance in both inputs, "
                            "or identical inputs if variance is zero.")
                return np.nan
    
    if method in ["pearson", "spearman"]:
        return np.corrcoef(vx_common, vy_common)[0, 1] if method == "pearson" else _spearman_corr(vx_common, vy_common)
    
    elif method == "fisherz":
        cor_val = np.corrcoef(vx_common, vy_common)[0, 1]
        # Ensure cor_val is within (-1, 1) for arctanh
        cor_val = np.clip(cor_val, -1 + np.finfo(float).eps, 1 - np.finfo(float).eps)
        return np.arctanh(cor_val)
    
    elif method == "cosine":
        # Cosine similarity
        norm_x = np.linalg.norm(vx_common)
        norm_y = np.linalg.norm(vy_common)
        if norm_x == 0 or norm_y == 0:
            warnings.warn("Cannot compute cosine similarity with zero vector")
            return np.nan
        return np.dot(vx_common, vy_common) / (norm_x * norm_y)
    
    elif method == "l1":
        # L1 similarity
        if np.any(vx_common < 0) or np.any(vy_common < 0):
            warnings.warn("L1 distance interpretation assumes non-negative densities.")
            
        sum_x = np.sum(vx_common)
        sum_y = np.sum(vy_common)
        
        if sum_x <= np.finfo(float).eps or sum_y <= np.finfo(float).eps:
            warnings.warn("Cannot normalize for L1 distance; sum is too small.")
            return np.nan
            
        x1 = vx_common / sum_x
        x2 = vy_common / sum_y
        
        # L1 distance is sum(|x1_i - x2_i|). Similarity = 1 - 0.5 * L1_distance
        l1_dist = np.sum(np.abs(x1 - x2))
        return 1 - 0.5 * l1_dist
    
    elif method == "jaccard":
        # Extended Jaccard similarity
        min_sum = np.sum(np.minimum(vx_common, vy_common))
        max_sum = np.sum(np.maximum(vx_common, vy_common))
        
        if max_sum <= np.finfo(float).eps:
            warnings.warn("Cannot compute Jaccard similarity; max sum is too small.")
            return np.nan
            
        return min_sum / max_sum
    
    elif method == "dcov":
        # Distance correlation
        sum_x = np.sum(vx_common)
        sum_y = np.sum(vy_common)
        
        if sum_x <= np.finfo(float).eps or sum_y <= np.finfo(float).eps:
            warnings.warn("Cannot normalize for dcov; sum is too small.")
            return np.nan
            
        x1 = vx_common / sum_x
        x2 = vy_common / sum_y
        
        try:
            return _distance_correlation(x1, x2)
        except Exception as e:
            warnings.warn(f"Error computing distance correlation: {e}")
            return np.nan
    
    else:
        raise ValueError(f"Unknown similarity method: {method}")


def _spearman_corr(x: NDArray, y: NDArray) -> float:
    """Compute Spearman correlation coefficient."""
    from scipy import stats
    return stats.spearmanr(x, y)[0]


def _distance_correlation(x: NDArray, y: NDArray) -> float:
    """
    Compute distance correlation between two arrays.
    
    Simple implementation of distance correlation.
    For better performance, consider using the 'dcor' package.
    """
    n = len(x)
    if n < 2:
        return np.nan
        
    # Compute distance matrices
    x_dist = np.abs(x[:, np.newaxis] - x[np.newaxis, :])
    y_dist = np.abs(y[:, np.newaxis] - y[np.newaxis, :])
    
    # Double center the distance matrices
    x_dist_centered = x_dist - x_dist.mean(axis=0) - x_dist.mean(axis=1)[:, np.newaxis] + x_dist.mean()
    y_dist_centered = y_dist - y_dist.mean(axis=0) - y_dist.mean(axis=1)[:, np.newaxis] + y_dist.mean()
    
    # Compute distance covariance and variances
    dcov_xy = np.sqrt(np.mean(x_dist_centered * y_dist_centered))
    dvar_x = np.sqrt(np.mean(x_dist_centered * x_dist_centered))
    dvar_y = np.sqrt(np.mean(y_dist_centered * y_dist_centered))
    
    if dvar_x == 0 or dvar_y == 0:
        return 0.0
        
    return dcov_xy / np.sqrt(dvar_x * dvar_y)


# Additional similarity functions for fixation groups will be added here

content.text: Now let me create a utility module for EMD computation:

input.content:
"""
Earth Mover's Distance utilities.

This module provides a wrapper for EMD computation with fallback options.
"""

import numpy as np
from numpy.typing import NDArray
import warnings


def emdw(x: NDArray, wx: NDArray, y: NDArray, wy: NDArray, lambda_reg: float = 0.01) -> float:
    """
    Compute weighted Earth Mover's Distance (Wasserstein-1) between two 2-D point clouds.
    
    This function tries to use various packages in order of preference:
    1. emdist (fastest)
    2. ot (POT - Python Optimal Transport)
    3. scipy.stats.wasserstein_distance (1D only)
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    x : ndarray of shape (n, 2)
        First set of 2D points
    wx : ndarray of shape (n,)
        Weights for first set (will be normalized)
    y : ndarray of shape (m, 2) 
        Second set of 2D points
    wy : ndarray of shape (m,)
        Weights for second set (will be normalized)
    lambda_reg : float, default=0.01
        Regularization parameter for Sinkhorn algorithm
        
    Returns
    -------
    float
        Earth Mover's Distance
    """
    # Normalize weights
    wx = wx / np.sum(wx)
    wy = wy / np.sum(wy)
    
    # Try emdist first (if available)
    try:
        import emdist
        return emdist.emdw(x, wx, y, wy)
    except ImportError:
        pass
    
    # Try POT (Python Optimal Transport)
    try:
        import ot
        # Compute cost matrix (Euclidean distances)
        M = ot.dist(x, y, metric='euclidean')
        # Use Sinkhorn algorithm for regularized transport
        return ot.sinkhorn2(wx, wy, M, lambda_reg)[0]
    except ImportError:
        pass
    
    # For 1D case, can use scipy
    if x.shape[1] == 1 and y.shape[1] == 1:
        try:
            from scipy.stats import wasserstein_distance
            return wasserstein_distance(x.ravel(), y.ravel(), wx, wy)
        except ImportError:
            pass
    
    # No package available
    raise ImportError(
        "EMD computation requires one of: 'emdist', 'pot' (Python Optimal Transport), "
        "or 'scipy' (for 1D case only). Please install one of these packages."
    )

"""Tests for similarity metrics."""

import numpy as np
import pytest
from numpy.testing import assert_allclose

from peyesim.core.fixation_group import fixation_group
from peyesim.core.eye_density import eye_density
from peyesim.similarity.similarity import similarity, compute_similarity


class TestComputeSimilarity:
    """Test compute_similarity function with arrays."""
    
    def test_pearson_correlation(self):
        """Test Pearson correlation."""
        x = np.array([1, 2, 3, 4, 5])
        y = np.array([2, 4, 6, 8, 10])  # Perfect correlation
        
        sim = compute_similarity(x, y, method="pearson")
        assert_allclose(sim, 1.0, rtol=1e-10)
        
        # Negative correlation
        y_neg = np.array([10, 8, 6, 4, 2])
        sim_neg = compute_similarity(x, y_neg, method="pearson")
        assert_allclose(sim_neg, -1.0, rtol=1e-10)
    
    def test_pearson_no_variance(self):
        """Test Pearson with zero variance."""
        x = np.array([1, 1, 1, 1])
        y = np.array([1, 1, 1, 1])
        
        # Identical vectors with zero variance should return 1
        sim = compute_similarity(x, y, method="pearson")
        assert sim == 1.0
        
        # Different constant vectors should return NaN
        y_diff = np.array([2, 2, 2, 2])
        sim_diff = compute_similarity(x, y_diff, method="pearson")
        assert np.isnan(sim_diff)
    
    def test_spearman_correlation(self):
        """Test Spearman correlation."""
        x = np.array([1, 2, 3, 4, 5])
        y = np.array([10, 30, 20, 50, 40])  # Monotonic but not linear
        
        sim = compute_similarity(x, y, method="spearman")
        # Should be positive but not perfect
        assert 0 < sim < 1
    
    def test_fisherz_transform(self):
        """Test Fisher z-transform."""
        x = np.array([1, 2, 3, 4, 5])
        y = np.array([2, 4, 6, 8, 10])
        
        sim = compute_similarity(x, y, method="fisherz")
        # Fisher z of perfect correlation should be large
        assert sim > 1
        
        # Test with moderate correlation
        y_mod = np.array([1.5, 3, 4, 5.5, 8])
        sim_mod = compute_similarity(x, y_mod, method="fisherz")
        # Should be finite
        assert np.isfinite(sim_mod)
    
    def test_cosine_similarity(self):
        """Test cosine similarity."""
        x = np.array([1, 0, 0])
        y = np.array([1, 0, 0])
        
        # Identical vectors
        sim = compute_similarity(x, y, method="cosine")
        assert_allclose(sim, 1.0)
        
        # Orthogonal vectors
        y_orth = np.array([0, 1, 0])
        sim_orth = compute_similarity(x, y_orth, method="cosine")
        assert_allclose(sim_orth, 0.0)
        
        # Opposite vectors
        y_opp = np.array([-1, 0, 0])
        sim_opp = compute_similarity(x, y_opp, method="cosine")
        assert_allclose(sim_opp, -1.0)
    
    def test_l1_similarity(self):
        """Test L1 similarity."""
        # Identical distributions
        x = np.array([0.25, 0.25, 0.25, 0.25])
        y = np.array([0.25, 0.25, 0.25, 0.25])
        
        sim = compute_similarity(x, y, method="l1")
        assert_allclose(sim, 1.0)
        
        # Completely different distributions
        x_diff = np.array([1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0])
        y_diff = np.array([0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 1.0])
        
        sim_diff = compute_similarity(x_diff, y_diff, method="l1")
        assert_allclose(sim_diff, 0.0)
    
    def test_jaccard_similarity(self):
        """Test Jaccard similarity."""
        x = np.array([1, 2, 3, 4])
        y = np.array([1, 2, 3, 4])
        
        # Identical arrays
        sim = compute_similarity(x, y, method="jaccard")
        assert_allclose(sim, 1.0)
        
        # Different arrays
        y_diff = np.array([0, 0, 0, 0])
        sim_diff = compute_similarity(x, y_diff, method="jaccard")
        assert sim_diff < 1.0
    
    def test_dcov_similarity(self):
        """Test distance correlation."""
        # Perfect dependence
        x = np.array([1, 2, 3, 4, 5])
        y = x ** 2  # Non-linear but perfect dependence
        
        sim = compute_similarity(x, y, method="dcov")
        assert sim > 0.9  # Should be close to 1
        
        # Independent
        np.random.seed(42)
        x_rand = np.random.randn(100)
        y_rand = np.random.randn(100)
        
        sim_ind = compute_similarity(x_rand, y_rand, method="dcov")
        assert sim_ind < 0.2  # Should be close to 0
    
    def test_nan_handling(self):
        """Test handling of NaN values."""
        x = np.array([1, 2, np.nan, 4, 5])
        y = np.array([2, 4, 6, np.nan, 10])
        
        # Should handle NaNs and compute on valid pairs
        sim = compute_similarity(x, y, method="pearson")
        assert np.isfinite(sim)
        
        # All NaN should return NaN
        x_nan = np.full(5, np.nan)
        y_nan = np.full(5, np.nan)
        sim_nan = compute_similarity(x_nan, y_nan, method="pearson")
        assert np.isnan(sim_nan)
    
    def test_insufficient_data(self):
        """Test with insufficient data points."""
        x = np.array([1])
        y = np.array([2])
        
        # Single point - not enough for correlation
        sim = compute_similarity(x, y, method="pearson")
        assert np.isnan(sim)


class TestSimilarityDensity:
    """Test similarity function with EyeDensity objects."""
    
    def setup_method(self):
        """Create test density maps."""
        # Create simple fixation groups
        self.fg1 = fixation_group(
            x=[100, 200, 300, 200],
            y=[100, 200, 300, 200],
            onset=[0, 500, 1000, 1500]
        )
        
        self.fg2 = fixation_group(
            x=[110, 210, 310, 210],
            y=[110, 210, 310, 210],
            onset=[0, 500, 1000, 1500]
        )
        
        # Create density maps
        self.density1 = eye_density(
            self.fg1,
            sigma=50,
            xbounds=(0, 400),
            ybounds=(0, 400),
            outdim=(50, 50)
        )
        
        self.density2 = eye_density(
            self.fg2,
            sigma=50,
            xbounds=(0, 400),
            ybounds=(0, 400),
            outdim=(50, 50)
        )
    
    def test_density_similarity_pearson(self):
        """Test Pearson similarity between density maps."""
        sim = similarity(self.density1, self.density2, method="pearson")
        
        # Should be high similarity (close patterns)
        assert sim > 0.8
        assert sim <= 1.0
    
    def test_density_similarity_spearman(self):
        """Test Spearman similarity between density maps."""
        sim = similarity(self.density1, self.density2, method="spearman")
        
        # Should be high similarity
        assert sim > 0.8
        assert sim <= 1.0
    
    def test_density_self_similarity(self):
        """Test similarity of density with itself."""
        sim = similarity(self.density1, self.density1, method="pearson")
        assert_allclose(sim, 1.0, rtol=1e-10)
        
        sim_cos = similarity(self.density1, self.density1, method="cosine")
        assert_allclose(sim_cos, 1.0, rtol=1e-10)
    
    def test_density_similarity_methods(self):
        """Test various similarity methods work with density maps."""
        methods = ["pearson", "spearman", "fisherz", "cosine", "l1", "jaccard", "dcov"]
        
        for method in methods:
            sim = similarity(self.density1, self.density2, method=method)
            assert np.isfinite(sim)
            
            # fisherz can be > 1
            if method != "fisherz":
                assert -1 <= sim <= 1
    
    @pytest.mark.skipif(True, reason="EMD requires optional dependency")
    def test_density_similarity_emd(self):
        """Test EMD similarity (requires emdist package)."""
        try:
            sim = similarity(self.density1, self.density2, method="emd")
            assert 0 <= sim <= 1
        except ImportError:
            pytest.skip("EMD computation requires emdist package")


class TestEdgeCases:
    """Test edge cases and error handling."""
    
    def test_unknown_method(self):
        """Test error on unknown method."""
        x = np.array([1, 2, 3])
        y = np.array([4, 5, 6])
        
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Unknown similarity method"):
            compute_similarity(x, y, method="unknown")
    
    def test_mismatched_shapes_handled(self):
        """Test that mismatched shapes are handled."""
        x = np.array([[1, 2], [3, 4]])
        y = np.array([1, 2, 3, 4])
        
        # Should flatten and compute
        sim = compute_similarity(x, y, method="pearson")
        assert np.isfinite(sim)
    
    def test_empty_arrays(self):
        """Test with empty arrays."""
        x = np.array([])
        y = np.array([])
        
        sim = compute_similarity(x, y, method="pearson")
        assert np.isnan(sim)

        assert_allclose(sim, 1.0, rtol=1e-10)
        
        # Negative correlation
        y_neg = np.array([10, 8, 6, 4, 2])
        sim_neg = compute_similarity(x, y_neg, method="pearson")
        assert_allclose(sim_neg, -1.0, rtol=1e-10)
    
    def test_pearson_no_variance(self):
        """Test Pearson with zero variance."""
        x = np.array([1, 1, 1, 1])
        y = np.array([1, 1, 1, 1])
        
        # Identical vectors with zero variance should return 1
        sim = compute_similarity(x, y, method="pearson")
        assert sim == 1.0
        
        # Different constant vectors should return NaN
        y_diff = np.array([2, 2, 2, 2])
        sim_diff = compute_similarity(x, y_diff, method="pearson")
        assert np.isnan(sim_diff)
    
    def test_spearman_correlation(self):
        """Test Spearman correlation."""
        x = np.array([1, 2, 3, 4, 5])
        y = np.array([10, 30, 20, 50, 40])  # Monotonic but not linear
        
        sim = compute_similarity(x, y, method="spearman")
        # Should be positive but not perfect
        assert 0 < sim < 1
    
    def test_fisherz_transform(self):
        """Test Fisher z-transform."""
        x = np.array([1, 2, 3, 4, 5])
        y = np.array([2, 4, 6, 8, 10])
        
        sim = compute_similarity(x, y, method="fisherz")
        # Fisher z of perfect correlation should be large
        assert sim > 1
        
        # Test with moderate correlation
        y_mod = np.array([1.5, 3, 4, 5.5, 8])
        sim_mod = compute_similarity(x, y_mod, method="fisherz")
        # Should be finite
        assert np.isfinite(sim_mod)
    
    def test_cosine_similarity(self):
        """Test cosine similarity."""
        x = np.array([1, 0, 0])
        y = np.array([1, 0, 0])
        
        # Identical vectors
        sim = compute_similarity(x, y, method="cosine")
        assert_allclose(sim, 1.0)
        
        # Orthogonal vectors
        y_orth = np.array([0, 1, 0])
        sim_orth = compute_similarity(x, y_orth, method="cosine")
        assert_allclose(sim_orth, 0.0)
        
        # Opposite vectors
        y_opp = np.array([-1, 0, 0])
        sim_opp = compute_similarity(x, y_opp, method="cosine")
        assert_allclose(sim_opp, -1.0)
    
    def test_l1_similarity(self):
        """Test L1 similarity."""
        # Identical distributions
        x = np.array([0.25, 0.25, 0.25, 0.25])
        y = np.array([0.25, 0.25, 0.25, 0.25])
        
        sim = compute_similarity(x, y, method="l1")
        assert_allclose(sim, 1.0)
        
        # Completely different distributions
        x_diff = np.array([1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0])
        y_diff = np.array([0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 1.0])
        
        sim_diff = compute_similarity(x_diff, y_diff, method="l1")
        assert_allclose(sim_diff, 0.0)
    
    def test_jaccard_similarity(self):
        """Test Jaccard similarity."""
        x = np.array([1, 2, 3, 4])
        y = np.array([1, 2, 3, 4])
        
        # Identical arrays
        sim = compute_similarity(x, y, method="jaccard")
        assert_allclose(sim, 1.0)
        
        # Different arrays
        y_diff = np.array([0, 0, 0, 0])
        sim_diff = compute_similarity(x, y_diff, method="jaccard")
        assert sim_diff < 1.0
    
    def test_dcov_similarity(self):
        """Test distance correlation."""
        # Perfect dependence
        x = np.array([1, 2, 3, 4, 5])
        y = x ** 2  # Non-linear but perfect dependence
        
        sim = compute_similarity(x, y, method="dcov")
        assert sim > 0.9  # Should be close to 1
        
        # Independent
        np.random.seed(42)
        x_rand = np.random.randn(100)
        y_rand = np.random.randn(100)
        
        sim_ind = compute_similarity(x_rand, y_rand, method="dcov")
        assert sim_ind < 0.2  # Should be close to 0
    
    def test_nan_handling(self):
        """Test handling of NaN values."""
        x = np.array([1, 2, np.nan, 4, 5])
        y = np.array([2, 4, 6, np.nan, 10])
        
        # Should handle NaNs and compute on valid pairs
        sim = compute_similarity(x, y, method="pearson")
        assert np.isfinite(sim)
        
        # All NaN should return NaN
        x_nan = np.full(5, np.nan)
        y_nan = np.full(5, np.nan)
        sim_nan = compute_similarity(x_nan, y_nan, method="pearson")
        assert np.isnan(sim_nan)
    
    def test_insufficient_data(self):
        """Test with insufficient data points."""
        x = np.array([1])
        y = np.array([2])
        
        # Single point - not enough for correlation
        sim = compute_similarity(x, y, method="pearson")
        assert np.isnan(sim)


class TestSimilarityDensity:
    """Test similarity function with EyeDensity objects."""
    
    def setup_method(self):
        """Create test density maps."""
        # Create simple fixation groups
        self.fg1 = fixation_group(
            x=[100, 200, 300, 200],
            y=[100, 200, 300, 200],
            onset=[0, 500, 1000, 1500]
        )
        
        self.fg2 = fixation_group(
            x=[110, 210, 310, 210],
            y=[110, 210, 310, 210],
            onset=[0, 500, 1000, 1500]
        )
        
        # Create density maps
        self.density1 = eye_density(
            self.fg1,
            sigma=50,
            xbounds=(0, 400),
            ybounds=(0, 400),
            outdim=(50, 50)
        )
        
        self.density2 = eye_density(
            self.fg2,
            sigma=50,
            xbounds=(0, 400),
            ybounds=(0, 400),
            outdim=(50, 50)
        )
    
    def test_density_similarity_pearson(self):
        """Test Pearson similarity between density maps."""
        sim = similarity(self.density1, self.density2, method="pearson")
        
        # Should be high similarity (close patterns)
        assert sim > 0.8
        assert sim <= 1.0
    
    def test_density_similarity_spearman(self):
        """Test Spearman similarity between density maps."""
        sim = similarity(self.density1, self.density2, method="spearman")
        
        # Should be high similarity
        assert sim > 0.8
        assert sim <= 1.0
    
    def test_density_self_similarity(self):
        """Test similarity of density with itself."""
        sim = similarity(self.density1, self.density1, method="pearson")
        assert_allclose(sim, 1.0, rtol=1e-10)
        
        sim_cos = similarity(self.density1, self.density1, method="cosine")
        assert_allclose(sim_cos, 1.0, rtol=1e-10)
    
    def test_density_similarity_methods(self):
        """Test various similarity methods work with density maps."""
        methods = ["pearson", "spearman", "fisherz", "cosine", "l1", "jaccard", "dcov"]
        
        for method in methods:
            sim = similarity(self.density1, self.density2, method=method)
            assert np.isfinite(sim)
            
            # fisherz can be > 1
            if method != "fisherz":
                assert -1 <= sim <= 1
    
    @pytest.mark.skipif(True, reason="EMD requires optional dependency")
    def test_density_similarity_emd(self):
        """Test EMD similarity (requires emdist package)."""
        try:
            sim = similarity(self.density1, self.density2, method="emd")
            assert 0 <= sim <= 1
        except ImportError:
            pytest.skip("EMD computation requires emdist package")


class TestEdgeCases:
    """Test edge cases and error handling."""
    
    def test_unknown_method(self):
        """Test error on unknown method."""
        x = np.array([1, 2, 3])
        y = np.array([4, 5, 6])
        
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Unknown similarity method"):
            compute_similarity(x, y, method="unknown")
    
    def test_mismatched_shapes_handled(self):
        """Test that mismatched shapes are handled."""
        x = np.array([[1, 2], [3, 4]])
        y = np.array([1, 2, 3, 4])
        
        # Should flatten and compute
        sim = compute_similarity(x, y, method="pearson")
        assert np.isfinite(sim)
    
    def test_empty_arrays(self):
        """Test with empty arrays."""
        x = np.array([])
        y = np.array([])
        
        sim = compute_similarity(x, y, method="pearson")
        assert np.isnan(sim)

"""
Core similarity metrics for eye tracking data.

This module provides various similarity metrics for comparing eye tracking data
including fixation groups, density maps, and scanpaths.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Union, Optional, List, Dict, Any
import numpy as np
from numpy.typing import NDArray
import warnings
from functools import singledispatch

from peyesim.core.fixation_group import FixationGroup
from peyesim.core.eye_density import EyeDensity


@singledispatch
def similarity(x: Any, y: Any, method: str = "pearson", **kwargs) -> Union[float, NDArray]:
    """
    Compute similarity between two eye tracking data objects.
    
    This is a generic function that dispatches to specific implementations
    based on the type of the first argument.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    x : object
        First eye tracking data object
    y : object
        Second eye tracking data object
    method : str, default="pearson"
        Similarity metric to use
    **kwargs
        Additional arguments passed to the specific similarity function
        
    Returns
    -------
    float or ndarray
        Similarity score(s)
    """
    raise NotImplementedError(f"No similarity method for type {type(x)}")


@similarity.register(EyeDensity)
def similarity_density(
    x: EyeDensity,
    y: EyeDensity,
    method: str = "pearson",
    saliency_map: Optional[Union[EyeDensity, NDArray]] = None,
    **kwargs
) -> float:
    """
    Compute similarity between two density maps.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    x : EyeDensity
        First density map
    y : EyeDensity
        Second density map
    method : str, default="pearson"
        Similarity metric. Options:
        - "pearson": Pearson correlation
        - "spearman": Spearman correlation
        - "fisherz": Fisher z-transformed Pearson correlation
        - "cosine": Cosine similarity
        - "l1": L1 similarity (1 - 0.5 * L1_distance)
        - "jaccard": Jaccard similarity
        - "dcov": Distance correlation
        - "emd": Earth Mover's Distance based similarity
    saliency_map : EyeDensity or ndarray, optional
        Saliency map for EMD computation with saliency weighting
        
    Returns
    -------
    float
        Similarity score
    """
    if method == "emd":
        return compute_similarity(x, y, method=method, saliency_map=saliency_map)
    else:
        if isinstance(y, EyeDensity):
            y_data = y.z
        else:
            y_data = y
        return compute_similarity(x.z, y_data, method=method)


def compute_similarity(
    x: Union[NDArray, EyeDensity],
    y: Union[NDArray, EyeDensity],
    method: str = "pearson",
    saliency_map: Optional[Union[EyeDensity, NDArray]] = None
) -> float:
    """
    Compute similarity between two arrays or density maps.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    x : ndarray or EyeDensity
        First data array or density map
    y : ndarray or EyeDensity
        Second data array or density map
    method : str, default="pearson"
        Similarity metric to use
    saliency_map : EyeDensity or ndarray, optional
        Saliency map for EMD computation
        
    Returns
    -------
    float
        Similarity score
    """
    if method == "emd":
        if not (hasattr(x, 'x') and hasattr(x, 'y') and hasattr(x, 'z')):
            raise ValueError("method 'emd' requires eye_density objects with x, y, z components")
        if not (hasattr(y, 'x') and hasattr(y, 'y') and hasattr(y, 'z')):
            raise ValueError("method 'emd' requires eye_density objects with x, y, z components")
            
        # Create coordinate grid
        coords = np.column_stack([
            np.repeat(x.x, len(x.y)),
            np.tile(x.y, len(x.x))
        ])
        
        wx = x.z.ravel()
        wy = y.z.ravel()
        
        if saliency_map is not None:
            # Extract saliency matrix
            s_mat = saliency_map.z if hasattr(saliency_map, 'z') else saliency_map
            if s_mat.shape != x.z.shape:
                raise ValueError("saliency_map dimensions must match density maps")
                
            # Compute residuals
            r1 = x.z - s_mat
            r2 = y.z - s_mat
            
            # Split into positive and negative parts
            pos1 = np.maximum(r1, 0)
            neg1 = np.maximum(-r1, 0)
            pos2 = np.maximum(r2, 0)
            neg2 = np.maximum(-r2, 0)
            
            # Compute EMD for positive and negative parts
            try:
                from peyesim.utils.emd import emdw
                emd_pos = emdw(coords, pos1.ravel(), coords, pos2.ravel())
                emd_neg = emdw(coords, neg1.ravel(), coords, neg2.ravel())
                return -(emd_pos + emd_neg)
            except ImportError:
                raise ImportError("EMD computation requires 'emdist' package")
        else:
            try:
                from peyesim.utils.emd import emdw
                emd_dist = emdw(coords, wx, coords, wy)
                return 1 / (1 + emd_dist)
            except ImportError:
                raise ImportError("EMD computation requires 'emdist' package")
    
    # Convert to vectors
    vx = np.asarray(x).ravel()
    vy = np.asarray(y).ravel()
    
    # Check for sufficient non-NA data
    valid_x = ~np.isnan(vx)
    valid_y = ~np.isnan(vy)
    valid_common = valid_x & valid_y
    
    if np.sum(valid_common) < 2:
        warnings.warn("Less than 2 common valid data points for similarity calculation.")
        return np.nan
        
    vx_common = vx[valid_common]
    vy_common = vy[valid_common]
    
    # Check variance for correlation methods
    var_x = np.var(vx_common)
    var_y = np.var(vy_common)
    
    if method in ["pearson", "spearman", "fisherz", "dcov"]:
        is_zero_var_x = np.isnan(var_x) or var_x < np.finfo(float).eps
        is_zero_var_y = np.isnan(var_y) or var_y < np.finfo(float).eps
        
        if is_zero_var_x or is_zero_var_y:
            # Check if vectors are identical
            if (is_zero_var_x and is_zero_var_y and 
                np.allclose(vx_common, vy_common, atol=np.sqrt(np.finfo(float).eps))):
                return 1.0  # Perfect correlation for identical vectors
            else:
                warnings.warn(f"Method {method} requires variance in both inputs, "
                            "or identical inputs if variance is zero.")
                return np.nan
    
    if method in ["pearson", "spearman"]:
        return np.corrcoef(vx_common, vy_common)[0, 1] if method == "pearson" else _spearman_corr(vx_common, vy_common)
    
    elif method == "fisherz":
        cor_val = np.corrcoef(vx_common, vy_common)[0, 1]
        # Ensure cor_val is within (-1, 1) for arctanh
        cor_val = np.clip(cor_val, -1 + np.finfo(float).eps, 1 - np.finfo(float).eps)
        return np.arctanh(cor_val)
    
    elif method == "cosine":
        # Cosine similarity
        norm_x = np.linalg.norm(vx_common)
        norm_y = np.linalg.norm(vy_common)
        if norm_x == 0 or norm_y == 0:
            warnings.warn("Cannot compute cosine similarity with zero vector")
            return np.nan
        return np.dot(vx_common, vy_common) / (norm_x * norm_y)
    
    elif method == "l1":
        # L1 similarity
        if np.any(vx_common < 0) or np.any(vy_common < 0):
            warnings.warn("L1 distance interpretation assumes non-negative densities.")
            
        sum_x = np.sum(vx_common)
        sum_y = np.sum(vy_common)
        
        if sum_x <= np.finfo(float).eps or sum_y <= np.finfo(float).eps:
            warnings.warn("Cannot normalize for L1 distance; sum is too small.")
            return np.nan
            
        x1 = vx_common / sum_x
        x2 = vy_common / sum_y
        
        # L1 distance is sum(|x1_i - x2_i|). Similarity = 1 - 0.5 * L1_distance
        l1_dist = np.sum(np.abs(x1 - x2))
        return 1 - 0.5 * l1_dist
    
    elif method == "jaccard":
        # Extended Jaccard similarity
        min_sum = np.sum(np.minimum(vx_common, vy_common))
        max_sum = np.sum(np.maximum(vx_common, vy_common))
        
        if max_sum <= np.finfo(float).eps:
            warnings.warn("Cannot compute Jaccard similarity; max sum is too small.")
            return np.nan
            
        return min_sum / max_sum
    
    elif method == "dcov":
        # Distance correlation
        sum_x = np.sum(vx_common)
        sum_y = np.sum(vy_common)
        
        if sum_x <= np.finfo(float).eps or sum_y <= np.finfo(float).eps:
            warnings.warn("Cannot normalize for dcov; sum is too small.")
            return np.nan
            
        x1 = vx_common / sum_x
        x2 = vy_common / sum_y
        
        try:
            return _distance_correlation(x1, x2)
        except Exception as e:
            warnings.warn(f"Error computing distance correlation: {e}")
            return np.nan
    
    else:
        raise ValueError(f"Unknown similarity method: {method}")


def _spearman_corr(x: NDArray, y: NDArray) -> float:
    """Compute Spearman correlation coefficient."""
    from scipy import stats
    return stats.spearmanr(x, y)[0]


def _distance_correlation(x: NDArray, y: NDArray) -> float:
    """
    Compute distance correlation between two arrays.
    
    Simple implementation of distance correlation.
    For better performance, consider using the 'dcor' package.
    """
    n = len(x)
    if n < 2:
        return np.nan
        
    # Compute distance matrices
    x_dist = np.abs(x[:, np.newaxis] - x[np.newaxis, :])
    y_dist = np.abs(y[:, np.newaxis] - y[np.newaxis, :])
    
    # Double center the distance matrices
    x_dist_centered = x_dist - x_dist.mean(axis=0) - x_dist.mean(axis=1)[:, np.newaxis] + x_dist.mean()
    y_dist_centered = y_dist - y_dist.mean(axis=0) - y_dist.mean(axis=1)[:, np.newaxis] + y_dist.mean()
    
    # Compute distance covariance and variances
    dcov_xy = np.sqrt(np.mean(x_dist_centered * y_dist_centered))
    dvar_x = np.sqrt(np.mean(x_dist_centered * x_dist_centered))
    dvar_y = np.sqrt(np.mean(y_dist_centered * y_dist_centered))
    
    if dvar_x == 0 or dvar_y == 0:
        return 0.0
        
    return dcov_xy / np.sqrt(dvar_x * dvar_y)


# Additional similarity functions for fixation groups will be added here

lines: -        if sum_x <= np.finfo(float).eps or sum_y <= np.finfo(float).eps:
-            warnings.warn("Cannot normalize for dcov; sum is too small.")
